Create a risk-of-bias assessment

Source: R/rob.R
reportilo_rob.Rd

Build a risk-of-bias object from a table of per-study, per-domain judgments, ready to plot with rob_traffic_light() / rob_summary() or export with reportilo_export().

as.data.frame() returns the wide judgment table (one row per study, one column per domain plus Overall).

Usage

reportilo_rob(tool, data = NULL)

# S3 method for class 'reportilo_rob'
as.data.frame(x, row.names = NULL, optional = FALSE, ...)

Arguments

tool

A tool_id (see rob_tools_available()).

data

A wide data frame: a study-name column (Study) plus one column per domain id (and optionally Overall). If NULL, a bundled example for the tool is used. Get a blank one with rob_template().

x

A reportilo_rob object.

row.names, optional

Ignored; for S3 consistency.

...

Ignored.

Value

An object of class reportilo_rob.

See also

rob_traffic_light(), rob_summary(), reportilo_export()

Examples

rob <- reportilo_rob("rob2")
rob
#> <reportilo risk of bias> RoB 2 (randomized trials)
#> 6 studies, 5 domains
#>    Study            D1            D2            D3            D4            D5
#>  Study 1          High           Low Some concerns          High           Low
#>  Study 2           Low Some concerns          High           Low Some concerns
#>  Study 3 Some concerns          High           Low Some concerns          High
#>  Study 4          High           Low Some concerns          High           Low
#>  Study 5           Low Some concerns          High           Low Some concerns
#>  Study 6 Some concerns          High           Low Some concerns          High
#>  Overall
#>     High
#>     High
#>     High
#>     High
#>     High
#>     High
#> Plot with rob_traffic_light() / rob_summary(); export with reportilo_export().
